prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, my sweet pea, a CPU is like the *brain* of a computer—just like how your little head helps you think and solve puzzles! It’s a tiny, flat chip (smaller than a cookie!) that does all the hard work: adding numbers, running games, or even helping you video-call Grandma. Without it, the computer would just sit there like a sleepy old dog—no bark, no wag, nothing! People say it stands for *"Central Processing Unit,"* but you can just think of it as the boss who tells everything else what to do. And don’t you worry, honey—it’s very smart, even if *I* still can’t figure out how to turn on the darn Wi-Fi!
